decision_process: Designation of a childcare-benefit recipient as fraudulent followed by full clawback of benefits
sampling_rule: Retrospective public-record reconstruction of the institutional decision architecture during the documented harm period; no individual claimant sample is asserted.

supporting_artifacts.citation: Parliamentary Inquiry Committee on Childcare Allowance, Unprecedented Injustice (Ongekend Onrecht), 17 Dec. 2020, English reference CDL-REF(2021)073-e: https://www.venice.coe.int/webforms/documents/?pdf=CDL-REF%282021%29073-e

requested_missing_artifacts: Contemporaneous case-file access logs showing what evidence reviewers opened before designation
substantive_findings.rationale: The inquiry record indicates that risk classification and institutional presumptions displaced access to case-specific evidence before designation.

supporting_artifacts.citation: Parliamentary Inquiry Committee on Childcare Allowance, Unprecedented Injustice (Ongekend Onrecht), 17 Dec. 2020, English reference CDL-REF(2021)073-e: https://www.venice.coe.int/webforms/documents/?pdf=CDL-REF%282021%29073-e

requested_missing_artifacts: Individual disposition rationales addressing alternatives, proportionality, and claimant circumstances
substantive_findings.rationale: The public reconstruction shows an all-or-nothing enforcement architecture with no demonstrated individualized weighing of relevant circumstances.

supporting_artifacts.citation: Parliamentary Inquiry Committee on Childcare Allowance, Unprecedented Injustice (Ongekend Onrecht), 17 Dec. 2020, English reference CDL-REF(2021)073-e: https://www.venice.coe.int/webforms/documents/?pdf=CDL-REF%282021%29073-e

requested_missing_artifacts: Reasoned decision orders and override records showing that named officials could direct otherwise in practice
substantive_findings.rationale: Formal administrative authority remained present, but the record does not show that it operated as an effective capacity to reject or modify the system-driven path.
substantive_findings.ambiguity_note: A scorer could argue for zero where formal authority was structurally unusable; this assessment records ceremonial authority as one.

supporting_artifacts.citation: Parliamentary Inquiry Committee on Childcare Allowance, Unprecedented Injustice (Ongekend Onrecht), 17 Dec. 2020, English reference CDL-REF(2021)073-e: https://www.venice.coe.int/webforms/documents/?pdf=CDL-REF%282021%29073-e
Dutch House of Representatives, follow-up record for Ongekend Onrecht: https://www.tweedekamer.nl/kamerstukken/plenaire_verslagen/kamer_in_het_kort/opvolging-rapport-ongekend-onrecht

requested_missing_artifacts: A harm-period appeal register showing timely, merits-based reversals and interruption of clawback
substantive_findings.rationale: Objection and appeal channels existed, but the public inquiry documents delay and limited practical effect during the assessed period.

supporting_artifacts.citation: Parliamentary Inquiry Committee on Childcare Allowance, Unprecedented Injustice (Ongekend Onrecht), 17 Dec. 2020, English reference CDL-REF(2021)073-e: https://www.venice.coe.int/webforms/documents/?pdf=CDL-REF%282021%29073-e

requested_missing_artifacts: Harm-period restitution, corrected records, notification, and named remediation ownership
substantive_findings.rationale: The assessed harm period contains no demonstrated remediation process for affected families; repair began after institutional and political intervention.

supporting_artifacts.citation: Parliamentary Inquiry Committee on Childcare Allowance, Unprecedented Injustice (Ongekend Onrecht), 17 Dec. 2020, English reference CDL-REF(2021)073-e: https://www.venice.coe.int/webforms/documents/?pdf=CDL-REF%282021%29073-e

requested_missing_artifacts: Harm-period authorization records changing risk criteria, thresholds, workflow, or the underlying enforcement architecture
substantive_findings.rationale: The public record does not show an exercised authority that changed the harmful decision architecture during the assessed period.

telemetry_integrity.provenance: The principal evidence is a parliamentary reconstruction with a stable public reference.
telemetry_integrity.completeness: The inquiry documented incomplete and withheld records; contemporaneous operational logs are not publicly complete.
telemetry_integrity.edit_authority: The assessed administration controlled many of the underlying records before external inquiry.
telemetry_integrity.independence: The later parliamentary inquiry was external to the operating unit, but the source records originated within the governed institutions.
telemetry_integrity.tamper_evidence: No public evidence establishes tamper-evident storage for the contemporaneous decision records.
telemetry_integrity.retention: Public materials do not establish a complete retention history for all relevant operational artifacts.
telemetry_integrity.rationale: The inquiry provides substantial reconstruction evidence, but missing and institution-controlled source records prevent an adequate integrity finding.

summary: The harm-period record shows absent counsel, judgment, repair, and reform, with command and correction preserved mainly in ceremonial form.
limitations: Retrospective public-record assessment; it does not reconstruct every individual claimant decision.
The period start is a conservative boundary for the documented harm architecture, not a claim that all relevant conduct began on one exact date.
The assessment evaluates documentary evidence and does not determine legal liability, institutional intent, or outcome correctness.
Post-2020 repair and reform are outside this machine-readable harm-period assessment.
